textPlain: Nice.  The iPads generally measure around ~8% slower than the MacBooks, I guess for cooling reasons.  So we should see approximately a 4400 single core Geekbench score for the MacBook series.  This is nice.Single thread MacBook progression on Geekbench:M1: 2350M2: 2600M3: 3100M4: 3850M5: 4400 (estimated)https://browser.geekbench.com/mac-benchmarks
I am very curious how GPU performance will be, especially for AI tasks
Lines up about exactly with the improvements in the A18 Pro i.e. ~+10% single and ~+15% multi.

Related, but not yet posted to HN that I know of:Leaked unboxing video reveals unannounced M5 iPad Pro in full - https://9to5mac.com/2025/09/30/leaked-unboxing-video-reveals...https://x.com/markgurman/status/1973048229932507518 | https://xcancel.com/markgurman/status/1973048229932507518Exclusive! Unboxing the iPad Pro with the M5 before Apple! - https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=XnzkC2q-iGI
And the GPU is up by about ~30%! This falls in line with the recent jumps in the A chips as well.
